steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that simulants "help scientists evaluate how well their devices will handle the surface when operated on Mars." The question asks what the passage says scientists use simulants for, so the correct choice must reflect testing equipment performance under Mars-like conditions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Scientists use simulants to track how the chemical properties of planetary soils have changed over time.': the passage says nothing about tracking soil changes over time. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Scientists use simulants to compare the physical properties of Mars's surface to those of Earth's surface.': the passage does not frame simulants as a comparison tool between planets. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Simulants can be mixed with soil from Earth to explore how research equipment will handle extreme terrains on Earth.': the passage is about Mars conditions, not Earth terrains, and does not describe mixing. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Simulants allow scientists to test the ability of research equipment to withstand some of the conditions it will encounter during a mission.': directly matches the passage — simulants let scientists evaluate how well devices handle surface conditions on Mars. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
